stat_of_week: 40 - Sugar Land Skeeters scored 40 runs in a single week, the highest run total of any team this season, powering an 8–1 demolition of Hiroshima Radiation
thriller_note: BaseballPro77 escaped with a one-category win despite being outclassed in runs, HR, RBI, and OBP, surviving on the strength of stolen bases and save/hold value — a result that keeps them near the top of the standings while Benchwarmers remain snake-bitten in close matchups.
key_storyline: Sugar Land Skeeters put together the most dominant offensive week of the season, plating 40 runs on a .346 OBP while Drake Baldwin, Matt Olson, and CJ Abrams all delivered across the board — resulting in an 8–1 thrashing of Hiroshima Radiation that wasn't even close. Meanwhile, Wisconsin Rapids Reapers quietly posted the week's best pitching performance (2.48 ERA, 0.84 WHIP, 75 strikeouts) and swept the hapless Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs 9–0, raising serious questions about Wonderdogs' roster depth heading into Week 3. The combination of Sugar Land's offensive explosion and Wisconsin's pitching dominance signals that the top of this league is separating fast.
lucky_reason: Benchwarmers won despite a luck delta of +0.58 — the highest in the league — meaning their actual win rate far exceeded what their category performance deserved, squeaking out a 5–4 result against BaseballPro77 with underwhelming pitching (4.89 ERA, 1.50 WHIP) and just 42 strikeouts.
unlucky_reason: General Hospital posted a sparkling 2.10 ERA, 1.02 WHIP, and a .363 OBP while winning their matchup, yet their luck delta sits at -0.52 — tied for worst in the league — indicating their strong category output has consistently been misaligned with their actual win-loss record across the season.
records_broken: The lowest ERA by a winning team record was tied: General Hospital posted a 2.10 ERA this week, matching their own record set in Week 2 (2.10, General Hospital).
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Back-to-back dominant weeks, 40 runs this week, elite pitching and offense firing together — clear #1 | same
2. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | 9–0 sweep with a 0.84 WHIP and 75 K's is impossible to ignore; Ben Rice emerging as a must-start | up
3. Riverside Roughnecks | 38 runs, 10 HR, and a 2.42 ERA in a 7–3 win; Xavier Edwards and Kerry Carpenter quietly carrying this roster | up
4. BaseballPro77 | Won again but the underlying numbers are shaky — low HR, high ERA; living on thin margins | down
5. General Hospital | Genuinely excellent stats being wasted by scheduling luck; Manny Machado and Kyle Tucker are elite anchors | same
6. North Correa | Oneil Cruz had a monster week (4 HR, 7 R) and the pitching held up enough; a legitimate contender | same
7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP is absurd; pitching staff was lights-out (2.25 ERA, 1.01 WHIP) in a 6–4 win | up
8. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge is doing his part but the team ERA (4.30) and WHIP (1.46) are dragging them down | down
9. Hiroshima Radiation | Carlos Correa and Kyle Schwarber are solid but 43 strikeouts from the pitching staff is a weekly liability | down
10. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison was brilliant (1.65 ERA, 26 K) but the offense couldn't back him up; busy waiver week shows urgency | same
11. Buttville Skidmarks | Decent pitching (3.19 ERA) but lost 7–3; offense lacks a true power anchor beyond Reynolds | same
12. Canberra Wombats | Jose Altuve (.577 OBP) and Christian Walker were excellent but pitching remains a structural problem | up
13. Benchwarmers | Lucky to win this week; pitching staff is a liability every single week and it will eventually catch up | down
14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Shut out 9–0 and scored just 16 runs with zero stolen bases; Pete Alonso posting a .233 OBP is alarming | same
waiver_highlights: Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | B | Added Masyn Winn(SS) and Dustin May(SP) | Winn provides speed and eligibility depth while May is a high-upside SP add after a brutal 9–0 loss — the Wonderdogs clearly hit the reset button and these are smart foundational moves

General Hospital | B | Added Kumar Rocker(SP); dropped Jack Leiter, Janson Junk, Cristian Javier | Consolidating three fringe arms into one high-ceiling starter makes sense for a team whose pitching is already elite — Rocker's upside is real

Albuquerque Bombers | B | Added Keaton Winn, Braxton Garrett, Noelvi Marte, Josh Jung; dropped four roster fillers | Aggressive multi-player waiver haul signals the Bombers are serious about patching holes; Jung and Marte add positional flexibility but the volume of moves hints at roster instability
North Correa | C | Added Austin Wells(C); dropped J.T. Realmuto(C) | Downgrading from Realmuto to Wells is a lateral move at best and a step back at worst — curious timing given they just won

Riverside Roughnecks | C | Added Victor Vodnik(RP); dropped Mark Vientos | Trading a productive 1B/3B bat for a reliever feels like a questionable positional sacrifice for a team already winning with offense
